stem: A application enters the immigration pipeline. The probability of passing document verification is 89%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing background check is 97%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing interview is 94%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing visa issuance is between 80% and 97% (uncertain due to evaluator variability). What is the overall probability that the application is approved?

ground_truth.answer: P(approved) ranges from 0.649 to 0.787 as the visa issuance pass rate varies from 80% to 97%. Point estimate: 0.722.

solution_steps: The application must pass all 4 stages sequentially: P(success) = P(stage 1)×P(stage 2)×P(stage 3)×P(stage 4).
The uncertainty in the visa issuance stage creates a 13.8 percentage point range in the overall success probability.
common_failure_modes: Adding probabilities instead of multiplying for sequential stages
Using a single value for the visa issuance rate without acknowledging uncertainty
Not recognizing that uncertainty at one stage propagates through all subsequent stages
